title: plectocephalus gayanus
intro: Plectocephalus gayanus, also known as Gay's plectocephalus or Gay's verbena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Plectocephalus gayanus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Plectocephalus gayanus can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used in some herbal preparations to support immune function and relieve respiratory issues.
